headline: Spark ALM Controller architecture documented; on-chain deployment addresses not directly surfaced
short_headline: ALM Controller system architecture identified; addresses pending

findings.text: No direct block-explorer name-tag search attempted; GitHub-based sources prioritized per remedial guidance
Spark Address Registry (GitHub) cited as canonical deployments source; direct fetch blocked, but architecture documented in ChainSecurity audit

ChainSecurity audit (2024-10-22, commit 6058f68f795) describes system contracts: ALMProxy (custody), MainnetController (Ethereum), ForeignController (L2s), RateLimits; no contract addresses tabulated in scope
GitHub sparkdotfi/spark-alm-controller confirmed; audits/ directory exists; direct fetch blocked by allowlist
Six chains documented (Ethereum, Base, Optimism, Arbitrum, Unichain, Avalanche); no per-chain address enumeration completed
No factory contracts identified in scope; SLL deploys to existing DeFi protocols (Aave, Morpho, Curve) rather than managing factory-spawned pools
Role taxonomy applied: MainnetController and ForeignController identified as admin/controller types; ALMProxy as proxy_admin; RateLimits as other
No on-chain addresses verified via block-explorer or defipunkd API; all findings sourced from audit PDF and docs

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice grade is always 'unknown'. The Spark Liquidity Layer's core onchain components (ALMProxy, MainnetController, ForeignController, RateLimits) are architecturally documented in the ChainSecurity audit and official docs, and governance structure (Sky Governance control) is confirmed. However, specific deployed contract addresses across six chains were not enumerated due to GitHub access restrictions and absence of a directly accessible block-explorer name-tag registry. The Spark Address Registry is the authoritative source but was not fetched. Subsequent slices will require on-chain verification of these addresses to complete the admin_addresses catalog.

evidence.shows: Security audits page lists Spark ALM Controller audits and links to audits repository
SLL documentation confirms system mints USDS/sUSDS through Sky Allocator Vault, bridges to L2s (Base, Optimism, Arbitrum, Unichain), and that Sky Governance controls all fund deployment

ChainSecurity audit dated 2024-10-22 (commit 6058f68f795) documents ALMProxy, MainnetController, ForeignController, RateLimits contracts; describes MainnetController for Ethereum mainnet (minting USDS, swapping via PSM, CCTP bridging); ForeignController for L2s (PSM deposits/withdrawals, CCTP bridging, ERC-4626 interactions); RateLimits for rate enforcement; DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE fully trusted, RELAYER semi-trusted

Official ALM Controller documentation confirms MainnetController and ForeignController roles, rate limits, and access control structure (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, RELAYER, FREEZER, CONTROLLER roles); governance spells deploy and initialize architecture

sparkdotfi/spark-alm-controller repository confirms this is the source repo for Spark Liquidity Layer onchain components; audit reports stated to be in audits/ directory; deployment documentation references mainnet and base production deployments

Deployments page references Spark Address Registry (https://github.com/sparkdotfi/spark-address-registry/tree/master/src) as canonical source for all Spark ecosystem contract addresses across networks

unknowns: D1: Block-explorer name-tag search not performed; no Etherscan/Basescan/Arbiscan queries executed for 'Spark Liquidity Layer' or 'Spark ALM' tags
D1: No Spark-tagged contracts enumerated from individual block explorers per chain
D2: Spark Address Registry (https://github.com/sparkdotfi/spark-address-registry/tree/master/src) fetch blocked; cannot enumerate per-chain deployed addresses
D3: Audit PDFs tabulate in-scope contract types but no instance addresses (ChainSecurity report describes architecture only, no address table)
D4: GitHub deployments/ directory and deployment JSONs not accessible; cannot extract mainnet or L2 controller/proxy addresses from artifacts
D5: Ethereum mainnet — MainnetController and ALMProxy addresses unknown
D5: Base — ForeignController and ALMProxy addresses unknown
D5: Optimism — ForeignController and ALMProxy addresses unknown
D5: Arbitrum — ForeignController and ALMProxy addresses unknown
D5: Unichain — ForeignController and ALMProxy addresses unknown
D5: Avalanche — ForeignController and ALMProxy addresses unknown (mentioned in DeFiLlama but not confirmed in primary sources)
D6: No pool/vault factory discovery required; SLL deploys to external protocols (Aave, Morpho, Curve) without spawning factory children
D8: No on-chain contract addresses verified via defipunkd /api/contract/read or block-explorer calls

protocol_metadata.about: Spark Liquidity Layer is the onchain capital routing system of Spark Protocol that automates the minting, bridging, deployment, and management of stablecoin liquidity (USDS, sUSDS, USDC) borrowed from Sky's reserves across six EVM chains (Ethereum, Base, Optimism, Arbitrum, Unichain, Avalanche) to DeFi and RWA protocols. It consists of ALMProxy (custody holder), MainnetController (Ethereum minting/swapping), ForeignController (L2 deployment), and RateLimits (rate enforcement). All fund movements and deployment strategies are controlled by Sky Governance.
